channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As he prepares to leave office, Biden urges incoming Democratic lawmakers to reach across the aisle
item.description: President Joe Biden is calling on incoming Democratic lawmakers to always be mindful of other people's perspectives — even when they may be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jan. 6: Security preparations in DC on anniversary of Capitol Riots
item.description: Jan. 6, 2025, marks four years since the Capitol riots when former President Donald Trump denied the election results.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich warns Freedom Caucus to study his era as conservatives issue demand letter following Johnson vote
item.description: Newt Gingrich engineered the Contract With America after forming the Conservative Opportunity Society, which some compare to today's Freedom Caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Melania Trump documentary from director Brett Ratner will be released by Amazon
item.description: Incoming first lady Melania Trump will be the subject of a new documentary directed by Brett Ratner and distributed by Amazon Prime Video.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Border shelters relieved the pressure during migrant surges. Under Trump, they could become a target
item.description: Republicans are stepping up their scrutiny of dozens of shelters run by aid groups on the U.S. border with Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Who is Giorgia Meloni? Trump hosts Italian PM at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is firebrand politician who is likely Trump's closest ideological ally in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers brace for Trump's promised Jan. 6 pardons. Some are urging restraint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fourth anniversary of the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol has a new focus as lawmakers brace for the prospect that President-elect Donald Trump may soon pardon many of the more than 1,500 people charged with crimes for their actions related to the riot.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No Jan. 6 disruptions are expected as Trump's win boosts Republicans' faith in elections -- for now
item.description: This Jan. 6 won't be the same. Four years ago, then-President Donald Trump urged supporters to head to the Capitol to protest Congress' certification of Democrat Joe Biden's victory in the 2020 election. “Will be wild!” Trump promised on Twitter a few weeks before Jan. 6, 2021. And it was.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ Dem slams New York as controversial NYC congestion pricing takes effect
item.description: New Jersey Democrat Mikie Sherrill blasted New York City's incoming congestion tax as she prepares her campaign for New Jersey governor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driving into Manhattan? That'll cost you, as new congestion toll starts Sunday
item.description: New York’s new toll for drivers entering the center of Manhattan is starting up after it withstood years of political wrangling, a last-minute pause and a flurry of legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pro-Israel Dem could tip scales in key Senate committee as Middle East war continues
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen, a new Democrat assigned to a key Senate committee, could bolster support for Israel in the new Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Congress will certify Trump's Electoral College victory on Jan. 6
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The congressional joint session to count electoral votes on Monday is expected to be much less eventful than the certification four years ago that was interrupted by a violent mob of supporters of then-President Donald Trump who tried to stop the count and overturn the results of a
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress is notified by the Biden administration of a planned $8 billion weapons sale to Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department has informed Congress of a planned $8 billion weapons sale to Israel, U.S. officials say, as the American ally presses forward with its war against Hamas in Gaza. Some of the arms in the package could be sent through current U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money criminal case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but indicated he wouldn't be jailed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Excerpts from the judge's ruling to go ahead with Trump sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge overseeing Donald Trump's hush money case in New York dealt with a host of weighty, unprecedented questions before deciding Friday that the president-elect should be sentenced later this month for the crime of falsifying business records.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House votes for rules to make ousting a speaker more difficult
item.description: The House of Representatives voted along party lines to adopt a new set of rules for the 119th Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol Hill's newest faces ready for bipartisan collaboration
item.description: Dozens of new lawmakers were sworn in, and Mike Johnson retained his position as House speaker Friday as Congress convened.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Mike Johnson clinches Speakership in stunning first ballot
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) was reelected to the top post in the House in a stunning floor vote on the opening day of Congress on Friday, securing the gavel on the first ballot. But the vote did not come without drama. Reps. Ralph Norman (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Mike Johnson wins Speakership on first ballot after 2 'no' votes flip
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) succeeded in keeping his gavel for the 119th Congress, winning in the House's Speakership vote in one round Friday. Johnson, who had a one-vote margin of error, won 218-215 over Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.). Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Which US elections are happening in 2025? Here's a look at upcoming primary and general contests
item.description: Most of the U.S. holds its major primary and general elections in even-numbered years, but there are a handful of places where competitive congressional, state and local-level contests will be happening in 2025.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI checks, ethics paperwork threaten to slow down Trump confirmations
item.description: Republican sources on Capitol Hill warn that President-elect Trump’s nominees may be delayed because of paperwork holdups and the slow start of FBI background checks, creating obstacles for the speedy confirmation of Trump’s national security team.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Conservatives seek commitments as Johnson scrambles for Speaker votes
item.description: Roughly a dozen hard-line House conservatives are demanding a host of amorphous commitments from Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to win their support Friday, throwing his leadership tenure into uncertainty less than 24 hours before the vote.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders gets Finance perch to push Social Security, Medicare expansion, tax reform
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), who supports expanding Social Security and Medicare and raising taxes on the wealthy, will get a seat on the Senate’s powerful Finance Committee, which has jurisdiction over those big issues.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation
